title: Phylloma × jacquinii
intro: Phylloma × jacquinii, also known as Phylloma jacquinii, is a hybrid plant species in the gentian family (Gentianaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Phylloma × jacquinii contains bioactive constituents like gentiopicrin, gentiogenin, and amarogentin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Phylloma × jacquinii is commonly prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to create remedies for respiratory and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Phylloma × jacquinii is a nothospecies of flowering plant in the saxifrage family Saxifragaceae. It is a hybrid of Phyllodoce empetriformis and P. caerulea. This hybrid is native to Europe, specifically the mountainous regions of the Alps. The classification of Phylloma × jacquinii as a hybrid species is based on its intermediate characteristics between the two parent species. This nothospecies is often found growing alongside the parent species in its native habitats.

benefits: Phylloma × jacquinii offers potential health benefits due to its rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. These compounds may possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. constituents: Phylloma × jacquinii contains a variety of active constituents, including terpenoids and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The plant's terpenoids, in particular, have been found to possess immunomodulatory and antitumor properties. Additionally, flavonoids present in Phylloma × jacquinii are known to have cardiovascular and neuroprotective effects.
